description_long: This sculpture is a tall, vertical stone carving featuring a partially revealed human figure emerging from a rough, unpolished block. The front displays a detailed depiction of a draped female form, with the upper torso, arm, and flowing garment visible, while the head and parts of the body remain embedded in the stone. The sides and back of the sculpture are left in a raw, natural state, emphasizing the contrast between the smooth, sculpted figure and the rugged, untouched rock. The base is a simple, rectangular plinth that supports the vertical mass of the sculpture. The overall appearance suggests a work in progress or a deliberate artistic choice to juxtapose the finished and unfinished. The coloration is predominantly beige to light brown, with subtle variations in tone due to the stone's texture.
description: A tall stone sculpture of a partially revealed draped female figure emerging from a rough, unfinished block, set on a rectangular base. The front is detailed and smooth, while the sides and back remain rugged and natural.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the rough, jagged stone surface with only hints of the sculpted figure visible, emphasizing the raw, unfinished quality.
description_view_1: The rear view displays mostly uncarved stone, with minimal detail and no visible human features, highlighting the natural texture of the material.
description_view_2: The right side view reveals the transition from the rough stone to the more defined sculpted area, with some drapery and body contours partially visible.
description_view_3: The front view presents the most detailed aspect, showing the draped female figure emerging from the stone, with clear definition of the torso and garment folds.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a neutral background.
